name: Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast
classification_notes: Oval-construction geometric sans with deliberately suppressed stroke contrast; the name and letterform samples confirm a geometric skeleton with even, low-contrast strokes throughout.
subfamilies: Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast Condensed
Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast Narrow
Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast Normal
Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast Wide
summary: A geometric sans built on oval forms with near-zero stroke contrast, available across four widths and seven weights.
description: Oval construction and flattened stroke contrast give this family a clean, even colour on the page across all settings. Four width variants — Condensed, Narrow, Normal and Wide — each carry seven weights from Thin to Black, yielding a large matrix suited to editorial systems and brand work requiring flexibility. The absence of italic cuts keeps the palette focused on weight and width rather than posture.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design offer Hurme Oval Sans Low Contrast as a multi-width geometric sans family, with width and weight axes spanning Condensed through Wide and Thin through Black, designed around oval letterform construction with consistently low stroke contrast.

name: Hurme Oval Sans High Contrast
classification_notes: A geometric sans with high stroke contrast and oval forms, spanning four widths and seven weights. The oval-based construction and strong contrast axis give it a display-oriented character distinct from neutral grotesques.
summary: A high-contrast geometric sans from Hurme Design built across four widths and seven weights, with oval letterforms that push the family toward display use.
description: The family runs from Condensed to Wide, with Thin through Black in each width, giving 28 static styles in total. Stroke contrast is consistently high across the range, lending lighter weights an almost flared quality and heavier weights a sharp, poster-ready presence. The oval construction keeps it feeling warmer than a strict modernist high-contrast sans.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Oval Sans High Contrast is presented across four widths — Condensed, Narrow, Normal and Wide — each available in seven weights from Thin to Black, offering broad flexibility for display and headline applications.

name: Hurme FIN 2a
classification_notes: The typeface features sharp rectangular counters contrasting with rounded outer shapes, giving it a highly engineered, geometric character. Its horizontal stroke terminals and stencil variants in lighter weights reinforce its geometric, technical classification.
summary: A geometric sans with sharp rectangular counters set against round outer shapes, with the three lightest weights drawn as stencils.
description: Based on the normal width of Hurme FIN 1, the design pairs rectangular interior counters with rounded outer letterforms to produce a distinctly technical, engineered aesthetic. The Thin, ExtraLight and Light styles are stencil cuts, adding a further industrial quality to the lighter end of the range. Short ascenders and descenders and horizontal stroke terminals keep it compact and structured across seven weights from Thin to Bold.
foundry_blurb: Hurme FIN 2a is derived from the normal width of Hurme FIN 1, distinguished by sharp rectangular counters contrasting with round outer shapes for a highly engineered and technical look. The three lightest styles are stencils, and the family features horizontal stroke terminals alongside short ascenders and descenders. OpenType features include stylistic sets, small caps, discretionary ligatures, arrows, fractions and a slashed zero.

name: Hurme Tek №2
classification_notes: A wide monolinear sans with hexagonal stroke terminals replacing conventional curves, placing it firmly in geometric sans territory with a mechanical and technical character.
summary: A wide monolinear sans distinguished by hexagonal stroke terminals in place of vertical curves, available in nine weights and as a variable font.
description: Hexagonal strokes replace conventional rounded curves throughout, giving the typeface a distinctly mechanical and angular character. A large x-height combined with short ascenders and descenders keeps it compact and legible at display sizes. Nine static weights run from Thin to Black, with a single-axis variable font also available.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe Hurme Tek №2 as a wide monolinear sans in a mechanical spirit, featuring hexagonal strokes instead of vertical curves, a large x-height, and short ascenders and descenders. It is available in nine static weights and as a single-axis variable font.

name: Hurme Tek №1
classification_notes: Described as a wide monolinear sans with mechanically cut horizontal strokes contrasting with round vertical strokes, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition with a technical, constructed character.
summary: A wide monolinear geometric sans with horizontally cut strokes, a large x-height and short ascenders and descenders, available in nine static weights and as a variable font.
description: Horizontally clipped stroke terminals set against rounded verticals give Hurme Tek №1 its distinctive mechanical rhythm, referencing technical and industrial lettering traditions. The wide proportions and large x-height make it well suited to display use and interfaces where legibility at size matters. Nine static weights from Thin to Black are complemented by a single-axis variable font for flexible deployment.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe Hurme Tek №1 as a wide monolinear sans in a mechanical spirit, characterised by horizontally cut-off strokes that contrast with round vertical strokes. It features a large x-height with short ascenders and descenders, and is offered as nine static weights alongside a single-axis variable font.

name: Hurme Tek №3
classification_notes: A strictly rectilinear, monolinear sans with no curves or diagonals, drawing on pixel-font and mechanical/digital traditions; its geometric rigidity and technical character place it firmly in the geometric sans category.
summary: A wide, strictly rectilinear sans built on the logic of pixel fonts, with no curves or diagonals anywhere in its construction.
description: Hurme Tek №3 takes the structural grammar of a pixel font and applies optical adjustments to make it functional at typographic scales. Every letterform is built from straight lines and right angles, giving the family a rigidly mechanical character suited to technical, digital and display contexts. Nine static weights run from Thin to Black, with a single-axis variable font also available.
foundry_blurb: Described by Hurme Design as a wide monolinear sans in a mechanical and digital spirit, Hurme Tek №3 uses the structure of a pixel font with optical corrections. It features a large x-height, short ascenders and descenders, strictly straight lines and right angles with no curves or diagonals, and is available in nine static weights alongside a single-axis variable font.

name: Hurme FIN Mono 1a
classification_notes: Described explicitly as monolinear and monospaced, with horizontal stroke terminals and a simplified geometric construction. Fits squarely within the geometric mono category.
summary: A simplified monolinear monospaced sans with horizontal stroke terminals, offered in seven weights with optically adjusted obliques.
description: Seven weights from Thin to Black, each paired with an optically adjusted oblique, giving 14 styles in total. The design is deliberately simplified, with monolinear strokes, horizontal terminals and shortened ascenders and descenders suited to tabular and technical contexts. OpenType features include stylistic sets, discretionary ligatures, arrows, fractions and a slashed zero; purchasers also receive the companion FIN Mono 1b, which sets a different default character set from the same underlying forms.
foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN Mono 1a as a simplified monolinear and monospaced sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders. The obliques are optically adjusted rather than simply slanted. Buying the 1a variant includes corresponding weights of FIN Mono 1b, an alternative design sharing the same forms but with a different default character set, with all alternates accessible via OpenType features.

name: Hurme Tek №4
classification_notes: A wide, monolinear sans with no curves and a mechanical/digital character, constructed from straight strokes and diagonals only, placing it firmly in the geometric sans category with a technical, modular sensibility.
summary: A wide, curveless monolinear sans built on a mechanical and digital aesthetic, available in nine weights and as a variable font.
description: Constructed entirely without curves, retaining only diagonals, this wide monolinear sans has a distinctly technical character suited to digital and engineering-influenced design contexts. A large x-height and short ascenders and descenders give it an even, compressed vertical rhythm across all nine static weights. Available as a variable font on a single weight axis, the family covers an extensive range of Latin-based languages.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe Hurme Tek №4 as a wide monolinear sans in a mechanical and digital spirit, with no curves but retaining diagonals. It features a large x-height and short ascenders and descenders, and is available as nine static weights and a single-axis variable font.

name: Hurme FIN Mono 1b
classification_notes: Explicitly described as monolinear and monospaced, with horizontal stroke terminals and optically adjusted obliques rather than true italics, placing it firmly in the monospaced sans-serif category.
summary: A monolinear monospaced sans in seven weights with obliques, offered as an alternate character-set variant to FIN Mono 1a.
description: Horizontal stroke terminals and shortened ascenders and descenders give this family a tight, technical character suited to interfaces and data-heavy contexts. The 1b designation refers to a different default character set from FIN Mono 1a, with the remaining characters accessible via OpenType stylistic sets; purchasing 1b includes matching weights of 1a at no extra cost. Fourteen styles in total cover Thin through Black with corresponding obliques, and OpenType support extends to fractions, ordinals, discretionary ligatures and a slashed zero.
foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN Mono 1b as a simplified monolinear and monospaced sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and optically adjusted obliques. It ships in seven weights across 14 styles, with OpenType features including stylistic sets, all-caps forms, discretionary ligatures, arrows, fractions, ordinals and a slashed zero. FIN Mono 1b is an alternative design to FIN Mono 1a, differing only in which characters are set as default, and buyers receive corresponding 1a weights free of charge.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Extra Narrow
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition. The extra narrow width is one of nine across the wider FIN 1a design space.
summary: An extra narrow cut of Hurme FIN 1a, a monolinear grotesque with eight weights and obliques drawn across a nine-width design space.
description: The Extra Narrow subfamily sits within the Hurme FIN 1a family, a simplified monolinear grotesque spanning nine widths from Compressed to Expanded and eight weights from Hairline to Black. Horizontal stroke terminals and a proportionally increasing x-height towards the narrower variants characterise the design. Each purchase of FIN 1a includes corresponding weights of FIN 1b, an alternative with angular, monospace-inspired characters and slab serifs.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe FIN 1a as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design space running from Expanded to Compressed. The family features horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and optically adjusted obliques, with the x-height increasing proportionally in narrower variants. Nine widths and eight weights yield 144 styles in total, with broad OpenType feature support and extensive Latin language coverage.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Condensed
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and optically adjusted obliques, placing it squarely in the grotesque tradition despite its extensive width-variant system.
summary: A condensed cut from Hurme Design's monolinear grotesque FIN 1a, offering eight weights with matching obliques as part of a nine-width, 144-style system.
description: Part of the larger Hurme FIN family spanning nine widths and eight weights, the Condensed variant shares the family's monolinear construction, horizontal stroke terminals and proportionally increased x-height relative to narrower cuts. Sixteen styles cover Hairline to Black with optically adjusted obliques throughout. Purchasing this variant also includes the corresponding weights of FIN 1b, an alternate version with angular, monospace-influenced characters and slab serifs accessible via OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design space running from Expanded to Compressed variants, featuring horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders. The Condensed cut is one of nine widths in a total family of 144 styles. FIN 1b, an alternative with angular monospace-inspired characters and slab serifs, is included free with purchase of FIN 1a in corresponding weights.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Compressed
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and no contrast, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition, here in its compressed width variant across a large design space.
summary: The compressed width cut of Hurme FIN 1a, a monolinear grotesque with eight weights and matching obliques drawn from a nine-width, eight-weight design system totalling 144 styles.
description: Hurme FIN 1a Compressed is one of nine width variants within a large monolinear sans-serif family characterised by horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and an x-height that increases proportionally as the width narrows. The compressed subfamily runs from Hairline to Black with a full set of optically adjusted obliques, giving sixteen styles in total. An alternative character set, FIN 1b, ships free alongside each purchase, allowing access to angular, monospace-inspired glyphs with slab serifs via OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN as a simplified monolinear sans-serif spanning nine widths and eight weights for 144 styles in total, with horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and an x-height that grows proportionally toward the narrower variants. FIN 1a and 1b share the same underlying fonts but differ in their default character sets, with the alternative glyphs accessible via OpenType; purchasing FIN 1a includes corresponding weights of FIN 1b at no extra cost.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Wide
classification_notes: Described as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals, placing it squarely in the grotesque tradition. The monolinear stroke contrast and systematic width variants reinforce this classification.
summary: The Wide width variant of a large monolinear grotesque family spanning nine widths and eight weights, with obliques and a companion FIN 1b included on purchase.

The family totals 144 styles across nine widths and eight weights, with the x-height increasing proportionally in the narrower variants. name: Hurme FIN 1a Narrow
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals, placing it squarely in the grotesque tradition. The monolinear stroke construction and large compressed-to-expanded design space reinforce this classification.
summary: A simplified monolinear grotesque in the Narrow width cut of a vast nine-width, eight-weight family from Hurme Design.
description: Part of the Hurme FIN 1a system, this Narrow variant offers eight weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching optically adjusted oblique, giving 16 styles in total. name: Hurme FIN 1a
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition. The family's range of widths and weights, alongside an alternative slab-accented variant (FIN 1b), reinforces its utility-driven, neutral grotesque character.
summary: A monolinear grotesque with nine widths and eight weights, forming part of a larger system that includes slab and monospaced variants.
description: Built on a simplified monolinear structure with horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders, Hurme FIN 1a spans 144 styles across eight weights and nine widths from Compressed to Expanded. Optically adjusted obliques accompany every weight, and the x-height scales proportionally in narrower widths to preserve legibility. Purchasing FIN 1a includes corresponding weights of FIN 1b, an angular alternative featuring slab-serif accents and monospace-influenced characters accessible via OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe FIN 1a as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design space ranging from Expanded to Compressed family variants, featuring horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and optically adjusted obliques. The x-height increases proportionally towards narrower widths, and the family totals 144 styles across nine widths and eight weights. FIN 1b, an angular alternative with slab-serif and monospace-inspired characters, is included free with any FIN 1a purchase.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Extra Wide
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and an optically adjusted oblique, placing it squarely in the grotesque tradition without humanist or geometric inflection.
summary: The extra-wide cut of Hurme FIN 1a, a monolinear grotesque spanning eight weights with obliques, part of a nine-width, eight-weight system totalling 144 styles.
description: Hurme FIN 1a Extra Wide sits at the broad end of a large sans-serif family that runs from Compressed to Expanded across nine width variants. The design is strictly monolinear with horizontal stroke terminals, short ascenders and descenders, and optically adjusted obliques rather than true italics. OpenType features include small caps, stylistic sets, oldstyle figures, tabular lining figures and a slashed zero; purchasing this family also includes the corresponding weights of FIN 1b, an alternative cut with angular, monospace-inspired characters and slab serifs.
foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN 1a as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design space spanning Expanded to Compressed widths. The family totals 144 styles across nine widths and eight weights, and buyers of FIN 1a receive the corresponding FIN 1b weights free of charge.

name: Hurme FIN 1a Expanded
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and consistent stroke width throughout, placing it firmly in the grotesque category. The expanded width variant sits within a large nine-width design space.
summary: An expanded-width cut of a monolinear grotesque family spanning nine widths and eight weights, with obliques and broad Latin language coverage.
description: Part of the wider Hurme FIN system, this expanded width variant offers eight weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching oblique, giving 16 styles in total. name: Hurme FIN 1a Extended
classification_notes: A simplified monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition. The design spans a large width axis across nine variants.
summary: A monolinear grotesque in the extended width cut, offering eight weights with obliques as part of a nine-width, 144-style superfamily.
description: The Extended variant sits near the wider end of the Hurme FIN 1a width axis, which runs from Compressed through to Expanded across nine distinct widths. Horizontal stroke terminals and short ascenders and descenders give the design a clean, utilitarian quality, and optically adjusted obliques accompany each of the eight weights from Hairline to Black. The family totals 144 styles across nine widths and eight weights, with extensive OpenType features including small caps, stylistic sets, oldstyle figures and a slashed zero. Buyers of FIN 1a also receive the corresponding FIN 1b weights free of charge.

name: Hurme FIN 1b Condensed
classification_notes: A monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and condensed proportions; the 1b variant introduces angular, monospace-inspired characters with slab-serif details, positioning it between a grotesque and a monospace design.
summary: A condensed monolinear sans with angular, monospace-inflected characters and eight weights across upright and oblique styles.
description: The 1b variant of Hurme FIN sits between the grotesque 1a and the Hurme FIN Mono families, using angular letterforms and slab-serif details on select characters as its distinguishing feature. Sixteen styles cover eight weights from Hairline to Black, each with an optically adjusted oblique. Purchasing FIN 1b includes the corresponding weights of FIN 1a at no extra cost, and the full FIN system spans nine widths totalling 144 styles.
foundry_blurb: Hurme FIN is a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design-space running from Expanded to Compressed. FIN 1b is an alternative to FIN 1a, featuring angular and monospace-inspired characters with slab-serifs, acting as a transition between the Hurme FIN Mono and the more grotesque FIN 1a. Both families share the same character sets, accessible interchangeably via OpenType features.

name: Hurme FIN 1b Extra Narrow
classification_notes: A monolinear sans-serif with horizontal stroke terminals and monospace-inspired angular characters; the 1b variant sits between a grotesque and a monospaced design, making it closest to a grotesque subcategory with slab-serif and monospace influences.
summary: An extra narrow cut of Hurme FIN 1b, a monolinear grotesque with angular, monospace-influenced characters and horizontal stroke terminals across eight weights.

Sixteen styles covering eight weights from Hairline to Black are provided, each with an optically adjusted oblique, and purchasing FIN 1b includes the corresponding FIN 1a weights at no extra cost.

name: Hurme FIN 1b Compressed

summary: A compressed, monolinear sans-serif with angular, monospace-influenced character alternates, running eight weights with obliques across a single compressed width.

foundry_blurb: Hurme describe FIN as a simplified monolinear sans-serif with a large design space running from Expanded to Compressed. FIN 1b is presented as an alternative to FIN 1a, featuring angular and monospace-inspired characters with slab-serifs as defaults, acting as a bridge between Hurme FIN Mono and the more grotesque FIN 1a. The family includes short ascenders and descenders, optically adjusted obliques, and an x-height that increases proportionally towards narrower widths.

name: Hurme Geometric Sans №1
classification_notes: Explicitly named as geometric in the typeface title and by the foundry; clean, uniform stroke widths and geometric construction place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A seven-weight geometric sans with true small caps, obliques and broad Latin language coverage, sold alongside a complementary No.2 variant.
description: Fourteen styles span seven weights from Hairline to Black, each paired with a matching oblique. True small caps and OpenType alternates give designers room to fine-tune the family for specific needs. Hurme Geometric Sans No.1 and No.2 share the same underlying drawings but differ in their default character sets, and purchasing No.1 includes the corresponding No.2 weights at no extra cost.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Design describe Hurme Geometric Sans No.1 as a seven-weight family with true small caps, matching obliques and alternate characters accessible via OpenType features. They note that No.1 and No.2 are essentially the same fonts with different default character sets, and that buying No.1 includes the corresponding No.2 weights free of charge.

name: Hurme Geometric Sans №2
classification_notes: The typeface is explicitly named and styled as a geometric sans, with consistent stroke weights and open, circular forms typical of the geometric category.
summary: A seven-weight geometric sans from Hurme Design with true small caps, obliques and broad Latin language coverage.
description: Fourteen styles span seven weights from Hairline to Black, each paired with a matching oblique. True small caps and alternate characters are available via OpenType features, giving the family some flexibility beyond its clean geometric core. It is closely related to Hurme Geometric Sans No.1, differing only in which character variants are set as default, with the other variants accessible through OpenType.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Geometric Sans No.2 includes seven weights with true small caps and matching obliques. Alternate characters and OpenType features make the family a versatile tool that can be fine-tuned to specific needs. It is essentially the same as No.1 but with a different set of default characters, and purchasing No.2 includes the corresponding weights of No.1 at no extra cost.

name: Hurme Geometric Sans №3
classification_notes: Named explicitly as a geometric sans and part of a series sharing the same basic construction and proportions; clean uniform stroke weight and geometric basis confirm the classification.
summary: A seven-weight geometric sans from Hurme Design with true small caps, obliques and broad Latin language coverage.
description: Part of the wider Hurme Geometric Sans series, all sharing the same underlying construction and proportions, this third instalment runs from Hairline to Black across fourteen styles including obliques. True small caps and OpenType alternate characters give the family additional flexibility for text and display work. The extensive language list covers well over a hundred Latin-script languages, making it a practical choice for multilingual publishing.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Geometric Sans No.3 includes seven weights with true small caps and obliques. Alternate characters and other OpenType features make for a versatile family that can be adjusted for specific needs. It is part of a series of font families sharing the same basic construction and proportions but each with distinctive qualities and features.

name: Hurme Geometric Sans №4
classification_notes: The typeface is explicitly named and styled as a geometric sans, with uniform stroke widths and clean construction typical of the genre. It sits within Hurme Design's broader Geometric Sans family series.
summary: A seven-weight geometric sans with true small caps and matching obliques, part of Hurme Design's four-family Geometric Sans series.
description: Fourteen styles span seven weights from Hairline to Black, each paired with an oblique, and the family includes true small caps throughout. OpenType alternates give designers room to adjust the face to specific contexts. The extensive language support covers a wide range of Latin-based scripts, making it a practical choice for multilingual European and international work.
foundry_blurb: Hurme Geometric Sans No.4 offers seven weights with true small caps and matching obliques. Alternate characters and other OpenType features make the family a versatile tool that can be fine-tuned according to specific needs. A specimen PDF is available for a complete overview of the typeface and its features.
